name: Extra Virgin Olive Oil

identity.basisReason: Per-100g as-sold basis matches oil labeling and avoids recipe dilution.

metricProvenance.header: USDA FoodData Central style extra-virgin-olive-oil entry, normalized to per 100 g as sold
metricProvenance.saturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.omega3_mg: derived fatty-acid field from the same source family; supportive rather than the main olive-oil argument
metricProvenance.polyunsaturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.cholesterol_mg: plant oil default, canonical expectation confirmed by source family
metricProvenance.vitamin_a_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_c_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_d_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_e_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_k_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_b12_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.iron_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.magnesium_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.zinc_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.calcium_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.potassium_dv: same canonical source family as header

pros.title: polyphenol reputation is a real plus
pros.explanation: It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile.

pros.title: contains useful polyphenols
pros.explanation: Adds extra value beyond the core fat chemistry alone.

pros.title: very simple ingredient identity
pros.explanation: A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

cons.title: extremely energy dense
cons.explanation: Still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast.

cons.title: quality varies a lot
cons.explanation: Source and authenticity matter in practice.

cons.title: easy to overpour without noticing
cons.explanation: A healthy image can make portion creep happen fast in practice.

scoreReadiness.summary: Core oil nutrition is clean enough for launch scripting, but the entry still treats quality and polyphenol strength generically rather than tying them to a specific bottle or harvest profile.
blockers: polyphenol-rich reputation is real but not quantified in the current schema
authenticity and storage quality vary meaningfully across real-world extra virgin olive oils

sourceNotes: Identity locked to plain extra virgin olive oil, per 100 g as sold.
Core nutrient values align with USDA FoodData Central style extra virgin olive oil entries.
Polyphenol/quality discussion is intentionally treated as context rather than a locked nutrient metric.
